title: Velvety Nostalgic Citrus Egyptian Stir-fry
description: A citrusy, velvety dish with a touch of nostalgia and red food coloring for added depth, featuring a dairy-free-friendly marinade and stir-fry technique.

ingredients.name: 1 can (14 oz) chickpeas, drained and rinsed

instructions: In a bowl, mix together lemon juice, Dijon mustard, honey or agave nectar, cumin, and cinnamon. Stir in red food coloring if using.
Add chopped bell peppers and shredded carrots to the marinade. Toss well until all vegetables are coated with the mixture. Let sit for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add enough vegetable oil to coat the bottom of the pan, about 1 tablespoon.
Add drained chickpeas and cherry tomatoes to the skillet. Cook, tossing frequently, until vegetables are heated through and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove vegetables from the skillet with a slotted spoon or spider skimmer. Set aside.
Return pan to heat if necessary. Add more oil as needed for stir-frying. Add carrots, bell peppers, and any remaining marinade back into the pan.
Stir-fry until vegetables are tender but still have some crunch, about 3-5 minutes. The amount of time can be adjusted based on preference for crispness vs. softness.
Combine cooked chickpeas, cherry tomatoes, and any reserved marinade with stir-fried vegetables. Toss to combine.
Serve hot as an appetizer or small dish. Garnish with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
